title: Spiced Plum Jam
description: My Thermomix take on a Matt Preston-style spiced plum jam — deep, glossy and gently spiced.
intro: This is my independent Thermomix homage to Matt Preston and the comforting appeal of plum jam. It keeps the spirit of the original inspiration, but the method is written fresh for TM6 and TM7 cooks who want clear settings and a warm, achievable result.
steps: Add the plums, sugar, lemon and spices to the bowl. Cook 5 min / 100°C / speed 1 to start.
Cook 25 min / Varoma / Reverse / speed 1, MC off, until thick and jammy (around 105°C).
Remove the spices, pour into warm sterilised jars and seal.
tips: A squeeze of lemon helps the jam set.
Star anise and cinnamon make it grown-up — lovely with cheese.

faq.a: Sealed jars keep for months; refrigerate once opened.

faq.q: Is this the original Matt Preston recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Matt Preston and plum jam; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
